hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images before fine-tuning on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the resulting transfer learning model will achieve higher classification accuracy across NCM compositions and aging states compared to models trained without pretraining, because the initial pretraining stage enables the network to learn generalizable morphological feature representations that compensate for the limited labeled data in the target domain.
